authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Bear River Reach A (Hwy 20 to Boardman Diversion Dam)
reaches.toDesc: Abandoned concrete dam (the Boardman Diversion Dam), 2.5 miles downstream of Highway 20

reaches.name: Bear River Reach B (Hwy 65 to South Sutter ID Diversion Dam)
reaches.toDesc: South Sutter Irrigation District Diversion Dam

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(9) — Bear River and tributaries (Placer Co.)
sources.quotedText: (9) Bear River and tributaries (Placer Co.) From Highway 20 south (downstream) 2.5 miles to the abandoned concrete dam (the Boardman Diversion Dam). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb. 5 trout

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(13) — Bear River and tributaries (Placer Co.) from Highway 65 to the South Sutter Irrigation District Diversion Dam
sources.quotedText: (13) Bear River and tributaries (Placer Co.) from Highway 65 to the South Sutter Irrigation District Diversion Dam. Fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 15. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — footnote definition of hatchery trout/steelhead (**)
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released. Wild trout or steelhead are those not showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is present).

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (CDFW booklet)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

regulations.humanSummary: Open Saturday preceding Memorial Day through the last day of February, on the segment from Highway 20 downstream 2.5 miles to the Boardman Diversion Dam.
regulations.verbatimText: From Highway 20 south (downstream) 2.5 miles to the abandoned concrete dam (the Boardman Diversion Dam). Sat. preceding Memorial Day through the last day in Feb.

parameters.counting_rule: 5 trout daily bag limit; this special regulation entry does not specify a distinct possession limit, on the Highway 20 to Boardman Diversion Dam segment.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit on the Highway 20 to Boardman Diversion Dam segment.
Open the fourth Saturday in May through October 15, on the segment from Highway 65 to the South Sutter Irrigation District Diversion Dam.
regulations.verbatimText: Fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 15.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies only to hatchery (adipose-fin-clipped) trout or steelhead; combined daily bag of 2, combined possession limit of 4, on the Highway 65 to South Sutter ID Diversion Dam segment.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag; 4 in possession, on the Highway 65 to South Sutter ID Diversion Dam segment.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Only hatchery trout or steelhead showing a healed adipose fin clip may be retained; wild (unclipped) trout or steelhead must be released immediately.

regulations.humanSummary: Wild (unclipped) trout and steelhead must be released immediately; only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery fish count toward the bag, on the Highway 65 to South Sutter ID Diversion Dam segment.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released. Wild trout or steelhead are those not showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is present).

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
